title: Evaluating Expertise of Concrete Pavement Construction in Modern Large Language Models
contribution: 评估26个LLM在混凝土路面施工领域的知识水平，提出三阶段评估框架（自动题目生成、模型评测、RAG域适应），RAG技术使所有五个选定的小/中型开源模型准确率超过75%，Phi-3 3.8B IT（量化）超过90%
abstract: This paper evaluates twenty-six large language models on their expertise in concrete pavement construction using over 100 automatically generated multiple-choice questions across five topics including subgrade preparation, base preparation, joint sawing/sealing, finishing and placement, and surface smoothness. The study proposes a three-stage framework combining automated question generation with GPT-3.5-Turbo, model evaluation, and domain adaptation via retrieval augmented generation (RAG), demonstrating that open-source small and medium models enhanced with RAG can achieve accuracy comparable to proprietary models while running locally on consumer hardware.
methodology: Automatically generates 101 multiple-choice questions from domain-specific concrete pavement construction documents using GPT-3.5-Turbo with one-shot prompting, then evaluates 26 LLMs on these questions across five topics, and applies a Retry parsing strategy and retrieval augmented generation (RAG) with additional documents to improve smaller open-source models without fine-tuning.
results: GPT-4o-mini achieved the highest overall accuracy (up to 90.9% on finishing and placement), while Llama 3 70B IT matched or exceeded proprietary models (up to 95.2% on subgrade preparation). With RAG enhancement, all five selected small and medium open-source models exceeded 75% accuracy, and Phi-3 3.8B IT (quantized) surpassed 90% accuracy, performing comparably to GPT-4o-mini on finishing and placement questions.
